content.body: So according to Google, `Math.fma()` is a function that already exists.

content.body: In our reality, I can only find a very brief discussion of introducing it here: https://web.archive.org/web/20141214112018/https://bugs.ecmascript.org/show_bug.cgi?id=309#c19
Though Temporal will mention the C++ `std::fma()` function in a note, step 11 here: https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-temporaldurationfrominternal

content.body: Context here is that waldemar suggests in https://github.com/tc39/proposal-amount/issues/115 that with such a function, we could get sufficiently precise conversion results in Amount.
